violations.rule: Table format: must have exactly 5 columns (# | Type | Front / Text | Back / Extra | Tags)
violations.detail: The table has only 4 columns — the 'Back / Extra' column is missing entirely. SKILL.md requires exactly 5 columns with one row per card, and explicitly lists omitting Back/Extra as a wrong format.

Cards 5 and 6 are a sacrifice/gain mirror pair. 'CP refuses requests to maintain consistency' trivially implies 'AP serves stale data to remain available.' SKILL.md explicitly lists 'CP sacrifices availability / AP sacrifices consistency' as a mirror pattern to catch.
MongoDB is CP and Cassandra is AP. With only two relevant classifications (CP or AP) and both cards adjacent, knowing one makes the other a coin-flip guess. These should be merged or one dropped.

Back reads: 'The tradeoff only applies during partitions — when there's no partition, you can have both C and A'. Contains the 'A — B' dash-elaboration pattern explicitly listed as a violation. The part after the dash is a second fact. Should be trimmed to 'The tradeoff only applies during network partitions.'

'MongoDB (with majority write concern) is classified as a {{c1::CP}} system' — the phrase 'majority write concern' hints strongly at consistency, making CP partially guessable from surrounding context.
